content.full_text:
August 19, 1976


Mr. Douglas A. Lewis
Attorney
Placer County Schools
1230 High Street
Auburn, California 95603

Dear Mr. Lewis:

Your letter of August 2, 1976, has been referred to me
for response.

You ask specifically:

"Who is the Code reviewing body of a General Law
County, Board of Education (Education Code §601, et seq.)
that exercises the duties and functions of the Board
of Supervisors in accordance with Education Code §§658
§606?1 and exercise jurisdiction in more than one
county?"

Several school districts including county boards of
education have inquired whether the Commission is the appropriate
code reviewing body for county boards of education when such
boards have supervisory responsibility over school districts
which extend into more than one county.

The Commission has determined that pursuant to Government
Code Section 82011, the Commission is the code reviewing body
for school districts which cross county lines. Government
Code Section 82011 states in pertinant part:

'Code reviewing body' means:

(a) The Commission, with respect to the Conflict
of Interest Code of a state agency, a county
board of supervisors, a city council or any
local government agency, other than a city
agency, with jurisdiction in more than one
county. . .

(Emphasis added.)

.3-19-76

The Commission has further determined that county
boards of education acting in a supervisory capacity over
multi-county school districts pursuant to Education Code
Sections 312.5 and 313, are not local government agencies
with jurisdiction in more than one county, as defined in
§2011 above; therefore the code reviewing body for a
county board of education is the county board of supervisors.

Thank you for your request. For further assistance,
please call Alfredo Flores, Conflicts of Interest Division
916/322-6444.

Sincerely,


Alfredo Flores
Staff Attorney, Conflicts
of Interest Division

AP:lhm

PLACER COUNTY SCHOOLS

Douglas A Leon, School Attorney          150 High Street    Auburn, California 95603
                                         916  823.2558


                                                          August 2, 1976


Fiar Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

          Re:  Request for an Advisory Opinion
               (Government Code §83114)

Gentlemen:

     Pursuant to the provision of Government Code §83114,
you are hereby requested to render your opinion upon the
following question:

     Who is the Code reviewing body of a General Law County,
Board of Education (Education Code §601, et seq.) that
exercises the duties and functions of the Board of Supervisors
in accordance with Education Code §§658 and 671 and exercise
jurisdiction in more than one county?

     Since a County Board of Education is a county agency for
some purposes, discharges some of the powers and duties of a Board
of Supervisors (Education Code §§658 and 671); and is also a
state agency under the control and supervision of the State
Department of Education, a Board of Education fits the
definition of both §82011 and §82041.  Thus it is unclear as to what
the proper Code reviewing body is.

     Your views on this matter would be greatly appreciated.

                                          Very truly yours,


                                          [signature]
                                          DOUGLAS A. LEWIS

DAL/ve

embedding.qa_text: August 19, 1976 Mr. Douglas A. Lewis Attorney Placer County Schools 1230 High Street Auburn, California 95603 Dear Mr. Lewis: Your letter of August 2, 1976, has been referred to me for response. You ask specifically: "Who is the Code reviewing body of a General Law County, Board of Education (Education Code §601, et seq.) that exercises the duties and functions of the Board of Supervisors in accordance with Education Code §§658 and 671 and exercise jurisdiction in more than one county?" Several school districts including county boards of education have inquired whether the Commission is the appropriate code reviewing body for county boards of education when such boards have some supervisory responsibility over school districts which extend into more than one county. The Commission has determined that pursuant to Government Code Section 82011, the Commission is the code reviewing body for school districts which cross county lines. Government Code Section 82011 states in pertinent part: 'Code reviewing body' means: (a) The Commission, with respect to the Conflict of Interest Code of a state agency, a county board of supervisors, a city council or any local government agency, other than a city agency, with jurisdiction in more than one county... (Emphasis added.) The Commission has further determined that county boards of education acting in a supervisory capacity over multi-county school districts pursuant to Education Code Sections 812.5 and 813, are not local government agencies with jurisdiction in more than one county, as defined in §82011 above; therefore the code reviewing body for a county board of education is the county board of supervisors. Thank you for your request. For further assistance, please call Alfredo Flores, Conflicts of Interest Division 916/322-6444. Sincerely, Alfredo Flores Staff Attorney, Conflicts of Interest Division AF: jhm PLACER COUNTY SCHOOLS Douglas A Lewis, Schools Attorney 250 High Street - Auburn, California 95603 (916) 873-4558 August 2, 1976 Fiar Political Practices Commission, 1100 K Street Sacramento, California 95814 Re: Request for an Advisory Opinion (Government Code §83114) Gentlemen: Pursuant to the provision of Government Code §83114, you are hereby requested to render your opinion upon the following question: Who is the Code reviewing body of a General Law County, Board of Education (Education Code §601, et seq.) that exercises the duties and functions of the Board of Supervisors in accordance with Education Code §§658 and 671 and exercise jurisdiction in more than one county? Since a County Board of Education is a county agency for some purposes; discharges some of the powers and duties of a Board of Supervisors (Education Code §§658 and 671); and is also a state agency under the control and supervision of the State Department of Education, a Board of Education fits the definition of both §82011 and §82041. Thus it is unclear as to who the proper Code reviewing body is. Your views on this matter would be greatly appreciated. Very truly yours, [signature] DOUGLAS A. LEWIS DAL/ve
